quiz.title: Unit 3 Quiz 9, Biology I
quiz.subtitle: Biochemistry: Lipids, Fatty Acids & Steroids
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Saturated vs. Unsaturated Fats, Triglycerides, Phospholipids, Steroids, Lipid Functions

questions.question: Which property defines lipids as a group?
options: They are polymers of repeating monomers
questions.explanation: Lipids are defined by their hydrophobicity — they are insoluble in water but soluble in nonpolar solvents. They are not polymers with repeating monomers (unlike proteins, carbohydrates, nucleic acids).

questions.question: Which type of fatty acid contains no double bonds?
questions.explanation: Saturated fatty acids have no double bonds between carbons — they are fully 'saturated' with hydrogen atoms. Unsaturated fats have one or more double bonds.

questions.question: Which fatty acid has exactly one double bond?
questions.explanation: Monounsaturated fatty acids contain exactly one double bond (e.g., oleic acid in olive oil). Polyunsaturated have multiple double bonds.

questions.question: Artificial hydrogenation of vegetable oils produces:
questions.explanation: Partial hydrogenation converts cis double bonds into trans fats. These artificial trans fats are associated with increased heart disease risk.

questions.question: A triglyceride is composed of:
questions.explanation: Triglycerides (fats and oils) consist of one glycerol molecule bonded to three fatty acids via ester bonds formed through dehydration synthesis.

questions.question: You are studying a newly discovered species and want to analyze its genetic information. What type of molecule would you analyze?
questions.explanation: Nucleic acids (DNA and RNA) store and transmit genetic information in all living organisms. Proteins, lipids, and carbohydrates do not function as genetic material.

questions.question: There is a coating on the feathers of waterfowl such as ducks that keeps them from getting wet. What molecule would this water-repelling coating be made of?
questions.explanation: The water-repelling coating on duck feathers is made of oils and waxes, which are lipids. These hydrophobic lipids prevent water from penetrating the feathers, keeping the bird dry and insulated. This oil is secreted from the uropygial gland (preen gland) near the base of the tail and spread during preening.

questions.question: Which of the following macromolecules contains C, H, O, N and P?
questions.explanation: Nucleic acids (DNA and RNA) contain carbon, hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen, and phosphorus. The phosphate group provides the phosphorus. Amino acids contain C, H, O, N but not phosphorus. Hormones vary widely; some are proteins, others are lipids. Carbohydrates contain only C, H, and O.

questions.question: The process of dehydration synthesis is known as:
questions.explanation: Dehydration synthesis is a condensation reaction in which two molecules are chemically joined, and a molecule of water (H₂O) is removed as a byproduct. This is the fundamental process for forming macromolecules like carbohydrates, proteins, and lipids.

questions.question: Animal fats tend to be solid at room temperature because they contain many:
questions.explanation: Saturated fatty acids pack tightly due to straight chains, making them solid at room temperature. Unsaturated fats have kinks from double bonds, preventing tight packing (liquid oils).

questions.question: Which lipid forms the main structural component of cell membranes?
questions.explanation: Phospholipids form the lipid bilayer of cell membranes. Their hydrophilic heads face water, while hydrophobic tails face inward. Cholesterol (steroid) modulates fluidity but phospholipids are the primary structural component.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a carbohydrate?
questions.explanation: Glycine is an amino acid (protein building block), not a carbohydrate. Sucrose (disaccharide), cellulose (polysaccharide), and glycogen (polysaccharide) are all carbohydrates.

questions.question: When basic units of carbohydrates, lipids, or proteins chemically combine to form larger molecules, which byproduct is always produced?
questions.explanation: Dehydration synthesis (condensation) releases water when monomers join to form polymers. This occurs in carbohydrate (glycosidic bonds), lipid (ester bonds), and protein (peptide bond) formation.

questions.question: Which of the following explains why sterols (steroids) are classified as lipids?
options: They are used as storage fats
questions.explanation: Sterols (steroids) are classified as lipids because they are hydrophobic and lipid-soluble, even though they do not contain fatty acids and are not primarily used for energy storage. Their classification is based on their insolubility in water and solubility in nonpolar solvents, which is a defining property of lipids.

questions.question: Which of the following, when it fails to occur, leads to a metabolic disorder in which the body cannot properly break down fatty acids?
questions.explanation: The oxidation of fatty acids is essential for energy production via β-oxidation. If fatty acid oxidation fails, the body cannot convert fats into usable energy, leading to metabolic syndromes such as medium-chain acyl-CoA dehydrogenase deficiency (MCADD).

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You understand lipid structure, types, and functions clearly.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Review saturated vs. unsaturated fats and triglyceride composition.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on what defines lipids and fatty acid saturation levels.
scoreMessages.0: Please review lipid classification, fatty acid types, and membrane components.
